description_long: This lamp features a striking starburst or sputnik design, with numerous elongated rods radiating symmetrically from a central metallic core. The rods are primarily translucent or frosted, likely made of glass or acrylic, and are tipped with metallic accents. The central hub is a polished metallic sphere, finished in a warm copper or bronze tone, from which all rods extend. The lamp is suspended from a chain, indicating it is designed to hang from above, and the chain matches the metallic finish of the core. The overall aesthetic is modern and decorative, reminiscent of mid-century or contemporary chandeliers. Its structure allows light to diffuse in all directions, creating a dramatic visual effect.
description: A modern, starburst-style pendant lamp with radiating glass or acrylic rods and a polished copper-toned central hub, suspended by a matching chain. The design is both decorative and functional, casting light in all directions.
description_view_0: Front view shows the symmetrical starburst pattern with the central metallic hub and evenly spaced rods radiating outward.
description_view_1: This view highlights the uniform distribution of rods and the chain attachment at the top, with the metallic core partially visible.
description_view_2: The rods appear evenly spaced around the core, emphasizing the lamp's spherical symmetry and the reflective metallic center.
description_view_3: This angle shows the dense cluster of rods and the warm metallic finish of the central hub, with the chain clearly visible above.
